title: Medical Documentation After Missed Exam Records
text: Students often ask what conditions allow medical documentation after a missed exam to qualify for a make-up exam. The records office accepts medical documentation after an exam to update attendance records. Documentation alone does not change a grade and does not create eligibility for a make-up exam.
label: lexical_decoy

title: Illness Make-Up Exam Policy
text: If a student misses an exam because of illness, the student may qualify for a make-up exam by submitting medical documentation within 48 hours of the missed exam. After verification, the instructor must offer a make-up exam window.
label: best_answer

title: Late Assignment Extensions
text: Medical documentation may support an extension for late assignments. The extension process for homework is separate from the exam make-up process.
label: partial_related

title: Travel-Related Exam Conflicts
text: Students who know about a travel conflict before an exam must request approval at least seven days in advance. Last-minute travel conflicts do not qualify for make-up exams.
label: wrong_condition

title: Weather Closure Exam Rules
text: When campus is closed for severe weather during a scheduled exam, all affected students receive a make-up exam date automatically. Medical documentation is not required for weather closures.
label: alternative_reason

title: Grade Appeal Deadline
text: Students may appeal final grades within ten business days after grades are posted. Appeals must identify grading errors and include supporting evidence.
label: irrelevant

title: Accessibility Testing Accommodation
text: Students with approved accommodations may take exams in the testing center. Accommodation letters must be on file before the exam date.
label: adjacent_but_not_answer
